cb_layout: c0:input(2pg) c1:scaler(1pg) c16:output(2pg) c24:max(1pg) c25:exp(2pg) c26:recip(1pg)
work_distribution: dim=-1: NC*Ht tile-rows, dim=-2: NC*Wt tile-cols

description: Reader/writer passthrough with compute identity copy. Establishes full data pipeline with TensorAccessor reads/writes, scaler tile generation, and copy_tiles identity.

approach: TensorAccessor for input reads, prepare_reduce_scaler for c_1, single pass streaming Wt tiles per row (dim=0) or Ht tiles per col (dim=1)
Identity copy using copy_tiles(c_0, c_16, total_tiles). compute_kernel_hw_startup(c_0, c_1, c_16) for init. tiles_per_work_unit = Wt (dim=0) or Ht (dim=1).
TensorAccessor for output writes, per-tile streaming from c_16. dim=0: Wt tiles per row, dim=1: Ht tiles per col with strided addressing.

cb_summary: c0: reader push 1/compute pop 1 (per tile, balanced). c1: reader push 1 (scaler, unused in stage 1). c16: compute push 1/writer pop 1 (per tile, balanced).

description: Compute applies exp to each input tile via copy_tiles with exp post_op. Single pass, no reduce. Only compute kernel changes.

approach: Added exp post_op lambda to copy_tiles: copy_tiles(c_0, c_16, total_tiles, [](uint32_t dst) { exp_tile_init(); exp_tile(dst); }). Added exp.h include.

cb_summary: c0: reader push 1/compute pop 1 (per tile). c1: reader push 1 (scaler, unused). c16: compute push 1/writer pop 1 (per tile). Same flow as data_pipeline, only DST post_op changed.

description: Full softmax dim=-1 with 3 passes: max reduce, exp(x-max)+sum+recip, normalize. Major compute kernel rewrite. Reader changes for 3-pass streaming. Writer unchanged.

approach: Changed to multi-pass streaming: num_passes=3 (stable) or 2 (unstable). Each pass streams same Wt tiles per row. CB backpressure synchronizes passes. Both dim paths updated.

Full 3-phase dim=-1 softmax: Phase1=reduce<MAX,REDUCE_ROW> on c_0->c_24. Phase2=sub<COL>(c_0,c_24)->c_25 with exp post_op + reduce<SUM,REDUCE_ROW> on c_25->c_26 with recip post_reduce_op. Phase3=sub<COL>(c_0,c_24)->c_25 with exp + mul<COL>(c_25,c_26)->c_16. Manual pop c_24,c_26 after each row. dim=1 path left as copy_tiles placeholder for Stage 4.

cb_summary: c0: 3*Wt push / 3*Wt pop (Wt per phase). c1: 1 push persistent. c24: 1 push from reduce / 1 manual pop. c25: Wt push+pop per phase (2x). c26: 1 push from reduce / 1 manual pop. c16: Wt push from mul / Wt pop from writer.

details: NoAccumulation not in scope - needs compute_kernel_lib:: namespace prefix

description: NoAccumulation is in compute_kernel_lib namespace, need to qualify it
evidence: compiler error: NoAccumulation was not declared in this scope; did you mean compute_kernel_lib::NoAccumulation

change: Qualified NoAccumulation with compute_kernel_lib:: namespace prefix

details: binary_op_init_common is not a member of compute_kernel_lib - it is in ckernel namespace (global). Also old build cache still had the NoAccumulation error.

change: Changed compute_kernel_lib::binary_op_init_common to binary_op_init_common (global/ckernel namespace). Cleared build cache.

triage: TRISC0=UABD TRISC1=MWDD TRISC2=K on both cores. NCRISC=CWFW BRISC=CRBW. Cryptic compute state.
suspected_cause: binary_op_init_common at top may corrupt hardware state for reduce helper that runs first

description: binary_op_init_common at kernel start corrupts hardware state for reduce helper. Reduce runs first and its reconfig may not fully recover. Remove binary_op_init_common since binary helpers (sub, mul) handle their own init internally (init=true default).
evidence: Hang in compute TRISC0=UABD TRISC1=MWDD. Writer waiting on c_16 = no output produced. First compute op is reduce which should work standalone.

change: Removed binary_op_init_common(c_0, c_24, c_16) call. Binary helpers handle their own init internally.

triage: Same TRISC0=UABD TRISC1=MWDD TRISC2=K. Removing binary_op_init_common did not help.
suspected_cause: Missing REDUCE_OP and REDUCE_DIM compile-time defines for compute kernel, OR compute_kernel_hw_startup wrong CBs

description: NoWaitNoPop for c_24 in sub may cause issues if CB internal state not properly synced. Try WaitUpfrontNoPop instead -- it will explicitly wait (instantly) for the tile and maintain CB state properly.
evidence: Consistent hang in TRISC with cryptic state. Same state whether binary_op_init_common is present or not.

summary: Per work-unit: Reader pushes 3*Wt tiles to c_0. Compute: Phase1 reduce pops Wt from c_0, pushes 1 to c_24. Phase2a sub pops Wt from c_0, waits c_24(NoPop), pushes Wt to c_25. Phase2b reduce pops Wt from c_25, pushes 1 to c_26. Phase3a sub pops Wt from c_0, c_24(NoWaitNoPop), pushes Wt to c_25. Phase3b mul pops Wt from c_25, waits c_26(NoPop), pushes Wt to c_16. Manual pop c_24 and c_26. Writer pops Wt from c_16. Total c_0 consumption: 3*Wt matches reader production.

issue: Deadlock: tile_regs sync forces all 3 TRISC threads to same phase. Phase 2a sub pack blocked on cb_reserve_back(c_25) while Phase 2b reduce unpack waits cb_wait_front(c_25). With 2 pages in c_25 and Wt=4 tiles to produce, pack fills c_25 after 2 tiles but cannot progress because tile_regs_wait blocks unpack from moving to Phase 2b to consume. Fix: increase c_25 from 2 pages to Wt pages so Phase 2a can complete fully before Phase 2b starts.

change: CB_EXP (c_25) from 2 pages to Wt pages for dim=-1 (Ht for dim=-2)
reason: tile_regs sync forces sub and reduce to run in same TRISC phase - c_25 must buffer full row of tiles

summary: With c_25=Wt pages: Phase2a sub packs Wt tiles (all fit), Phase2b reduce unpacks Wt tiles (all available), Phase3a sub packs Wt (c_25 was emptied), Phase3b mul unpacks Wt. No deadlock possible. c_0(2pg) c_24(1pg) c_25(Wt pg) c_26(1pg) c_16(2pg) all balanced.

fix: CB_EXP from 2 pages to Wt pages to prevent tile_regs deadlock
